summary: Of the 10 problems in first_proof_1, seven (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) appear fully resolved with a single accepted proof each, while three (q4, q5, q6) remain active research fronts. q6 is closest to completion with only 1 open issue against 15 resolved, whereas q4 (22 open) and q5 (14 open) carry the bulk of remaining mathematical risk.
problems: q4 has the highest open-issue load (22 open, 23 resolved), centered on limiting arguments from simple to general roots, implicit differentiation in Lemma 1, and a dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J) in the Hessian computation
q5 has 14 open issues involving converse direction connectivity bookkeeping, well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum, and an unjustified 'discrete slices' conclusion
q6 retains 1 open issue: a missing rank argument that the top-sigma bar term is controlled, related to earlier resolved barrier-update and trace-identity concerns
Recurring open-issue patterns across q4-q6 are rigor gaps in limiting/generalization steps, linear-algebra dimension and rank bookkeeping, and well-definedness/existence claims stated without proof
highlights: Seven of ten problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) have complete, accepted proofs with zero open issues
q6 shows strong traction: 15 issues resolved and only 1 remaining, indicating imminent closure
q4 demonstrates high engagement with 23 issues already resolved, showing active and productive review momentum
The open issues are well-localized to specific lemmas and computations rather than fundamental approach failures, suggesting they are tractable

suggested_todos.title: Close q6 by supplying the missing rank argument bounding the top-sigma bar term
Resolve the JJ* vs J*J dimensional error in the q4 Hessian and validate downstream Lemma 1 implicit differentiation
Tackle the q4 limiting argument from simple roots to general roots, the largest conceptual open item
Establish well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum and repair the 'discrete slices' conclusion in q5
Triage and batch-categorize the remaining q4 and q5 open issues by type (limiting, linear-algebra, well-definedness) to parallelize work
Spot-audit the seven resolved problems for archival completeness before final sign-off
